mechanism_narrative: A 5% YoY growth in real GDP signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of 5% YoY GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% YoY decline in industrial production signals a supply-side shock that reduces capacity utilization, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of 10% YoY industrial production decline are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 50-bps increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of 50-bps FEDFUNDS increases are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% MoM increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market that boosts risk aversion, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of 0.5% MoM unemployment claims increases are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% YoY growth in real disposable personal income signals a strengthening consumer sector that boosts equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of 10% YoY real disposable personal income growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% YoY decline in housing starts signals a supply-side shock that reduces capacity utilization, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of 5% YoY housing starts decline are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 25-bps decrease in the federal funds rate signals a easing monetary cycle that boosts equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of 25-bps FEDFUNDS decreases are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.2% MoM increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market that boosts risk aversion, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of 0.2% MoM unemployment rate increases are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
